title: Missing support for a wire/reg named "do"
body:
Working in a project, I had to handle with a 8b10b encoder/decoder. One of the port name is a port named "do" (I saw it in different implementations using the same name).

It seems iverilog does not support it and returns an error:

```
encode.v:47: syntax error
encode.v:47: error: invalid module item.
```

To reproduce the issue you can download these files and try to simulate with iverilog:
http://asics.chuckbenz.com/encode.v
http://asics.chuckbenz.com/decode.v
http://asics.chuckbenz.com/validate_8b10b.v
http://asics.chuckbenz.com/8b10b_a.mem

I don't know if the VERILOG implementation mark it as a reserved word. I noticed it is used, so it means that some compilers handle it as a valid identifier.

Best regards
Michele Renda
code_blocks:
encode.v:47: syntax error
encode.v:47: error: invalid module item.
